prompt: Each box shows an activity, its duration, and a computed TOTAL FLOAT (f). Do the forward and backward pass — one float is WRONG. Tap the activity with the incorrect float. (Total float = LS − ES; critical-path activities have zero float.)
caption: Activity-on-node network: A→B, A→C, B→D, C→D.

derived.shown: [errNode==0 ? fl[0]+errDelta : fl[0], errNode==1 ? fl[1]+errDelta : fl[1], errNode==2 ? fl[2]+errDelta : fl[2], errNode==3 ? fl[3]+errDelta : fl[3]]

regions.label: 'A'
regions.why: errNode==0 ? ('Shown f=' + shown[0] + ', but A is on the critical path so its float is 0. This is the error.') : 'A is critical (float 0) — shown correctly.'

regions.label: 'B'
regions.why: errNode==1 ? ('Shown f=' + shown[1] + ', but total float = LS − ES = ' + fl[1] + '. This is the error.') : ('B float ' + fl[1] + ' is correct.')

regions.label: 'C'
regions.why: errNode==2 ? ('Shown f=' + shown[2] + ', but total float = LS − ES = ' + fl[2] + '. This is the error.') : ('C float ' + fl[2] + ' is correct.')

regions.label: 'D'
regions.why: errNode==3 ? ('Shown f=' + shown[3] + ', but D is the last activity on the critical path so its float is 0. This is the error.') : 'D is critical (float 0) — shown correctly.'

explanation: Forward pass gives ES/EF; backward pass from the {{T}}-unit finish gives LS/LF; total float = LS − ES. The critical path (A and D always, plus the longer of B/C) has zero float; the shorter branch carries {{ abs(dB - dC) }} unit(s). One shown float didn't match — that was the error.
